summary.intent: Evolve Gossamer from a single-repo hardcoded SQLite setup to a multi-repo architecture where each repo has its own SQLite database, managed via a JSON config file at ~/.gossamer/config.json, with a UI to onboard repos and view cross-repo checkpoint/session data.
summary.outcome: Multi-repo architecture fully implemented: config.ts manages repo registry, each repo gets its own SQLite db with schema pushed on onboarding, server indexes all repos in parallel, UI has repo onboarding modal and a togglable Sessions/Repos view with branch-log drill-down. Several bugs fixed including cross-repo checkpoint lookup, config path mismatches, and CheckpointDetail rendering.

repo: Each repo gets its own SQLite database; the config registry at ~/.gossamer/config.json maps localPath → dbPath. The server's primary db (originally ~/.claude/hook-handler.db) must be reused for the gossamer repo itself rather than generating a new empty one.
CheckpointIdGitOidJoin table maps git commit OIDs to checkpoint IDs — this is the join needed to implement branch-log views (git log → OIDs → checkpoint IDs → sessions + log events).
The entire/checkpoints/v1 git branch is used for checkpoint worktree indexing; ensureWorktree sets up this worktree in .gossamer/checkpoints.
Session log events go through a shared rendering pipeline: logEventsToEvents → groupEvents → groupClaudeTurns → render, exported from SessionDetail.tsx for reuse.
The Electron renderer uses React Router MemoryRouter with routes for /sessions, /checkpoints/:id, /branch-log, /repos.
repoName on the Session type comes from basename(repoDir) on the server side.

code.finding: repoForCheckpoint() helper searches primary db first then iterates all configured repo dbs to resolve a checkpoint ID to the correct PrismaClient + localPath. Required because /api/v2/checkpoints/:id, /diff, and /diff-stats all previously only searched the primary db.

POST /api/repos dbPath resolution order: existing entry's dbPath → server's own dbPath when localPath===repoDir → defaultDbPath(name). This prevents orphaning existing data when re-onboarding the server's own repo.
Uses realpathSync to normalize paths before comparing localPath to repoDir, preventing symlink mismatches (stsoucas vs stephanostsoucas home directory).
ensureWorktree must be inside the try/catch of indexCheckpointsForRepo — if called outside, EACCES from mkdirSync bubbles up uncaught and crashes the entire server process.

Mantine TextInput's synthetic event has e.currentTarget nulled out by the time a functional setState updater runs. Must capture const v = e.currentTarget.value synchronously before passing to setForm(f => ...).

workflow: prisma db push --skip-generate is idempotent — safe to call on an already-migrated db without regenerating the Prisma client.
mkdirSync with { recursive: true } is a no-op if the directory already exists — no need for an existsSync guard.
When firing background work from an API handler (e.g., post-onboarding indexing), use void promise to return the response immediately while work continues in the background.
git branch --show-current returns empty string on detached HEAD; need git symbolic-ref --short HEAD as fallback.

friction: Gossamer repo showed no data because config.json had wrong localPath (/Users/stsoucas vs /Users/stephanostsoucas) and pointed to a freshly-created empty db instead of the real ~/.claude/hook-handler.db.
The /api/v2/checkpoints/:id route returned 404 for cross-repo checkpoints because it only searched the primary db — required adding the repoForCheckpoint() multi-db search helper.

CheckpointDetail went through three rewrites: first over-stripped (removed messages call but showed nothing), then restored with diff2html, then finally rewritten to match SessionDetail's expanded CheckpointRow structure.
React synthetic event pooling caused a null pointer crash in Repos.tsx — non-obvious because it only triggers with functional state updaters.
EACCES crash on server startup when indexing a repo the server doesn't have write access to — ensureWorktree was outside the error boundary.
The /api/checkpoints/:id/messages route was referenced by CheckpointDetail but never implemented on the server.
config.json localPath/dbPath mismatch between symlinked and real home directory paths required both a config fix and a realpathSync normalization in serve.ts.
open_items: The Repos UI onboarding flow derives dbPath automatically — there is no way for the user to specify a custom dbPath for repos that already have an existing database at a non-default location.
The /api/repos/status latestCheckpointId comes from checkpointMetadata.findFirst — for newly onboarded repos with no indexed checkpoints yet, this will show null until the first poller tick runs.
BranchLog screen exists but is only reachable by clicking a branch name in the Repos table — no direct navigation or search.
The WS change poller iterates all repos every tick; for large numbers of repos this could become a performance issue.
pushSchema errors in POST /api/repos are logged as warnings and swallowed — a failed schema push could leave a repo with an unusable db without the user knowing.
indexCheckpointsForRepo and indexShadowsForRepo open a new getDb() connection each tick rather than caching connections per repo.
